mission.title: falco: Support arm64 architecture in dockerhub
mission.description: Support arm64 architecture in dockerhub. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current falco deployment
steps.description:
Verify your falco version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
helm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working falco installation.

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
**Motivation**
Tried to run Falco in a arm64 AWS EC2 AMI **amazon-eks-arm64-node-***.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support arm64 architecture in dockerhub
steps.description:
New dockerfiles are multiplatform ready: they can be pushed with `docker buildx` for various architectures.
Moreover, updated falco-driver-loader to support multiple architectures.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support arm64 architecture in dockerhub" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
New dockerfiles are multiplatform ready: they can be pushed with `docker buildx` for various architectures.
Moreover, updated falco-driver-loader to support multiple architectures.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
